proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_015 is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear correlation).
Higher feature_014 (3-level ordinal) is associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotone gradient (worse PFS as feature_014 increases from 0 to 2).
Patients with feature_023=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_023=0.
Patients with feature_011=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_011=0.
Patients with feature_006='squamous' have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_006='adenocarcinoma'.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson correlation of feature_015 with pfs_months is r=+0.7571 (p<1e-300), confirming a strong positive linear relationship.
Mean pfs_months by feature_014: level 0 = 4.33 (n large), level 1 = 3.19, level 2 = 2.15. ANOVA F=3141, p<1e-300; clean monotone decline of ~1.1 months per level.
feature_023=1 mean pfs=2.92 vs feature_023=0 mean pfs=4.36. ANOVA F=5224, p<1e-300; effect size ~ -1.44 months.
feature_011=1 mean pfs=2.74 vs feature_011=0 mean pfs=3.66; ANOVA F=1653, p<1e-300; effect ~ -0.92 months.
Squamous mean pfs=2.81 vs adenocarcinoma mean pfs=3.67; F=1522, p<1e-300; effect ~ -0.86 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_016=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_016=0.
Current smokers (feature_001='current') have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers.
Higher feature_017 is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation).
Higher feature_025 is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
Patients with feature_028=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_028=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_016=1 mean pfs=4.11 vs feature_016=0 mean pfs=3.33; F=690, p~5e-151. Positive prognostic indicator (~+0.78 months).
Mean pfs by smoking: never=3.67, former=3.61, current=2.99. ANOVA F=433, p~4e-187. Current smoking is the dominant negative effect (~ -0.65 months vs never).
Pearson r between feature_017 (continuous, 0-28) and pfs_months = -0.127 (p~7e-179). Modest negative association.
Pearson r between feature_025 (continuous, 1.7-5.5) and pfs_months = +0.0991 (p~3e-109). Modest positive association.
feature_028=1 mean pfs=2.66 vs 0 mean pfs=3.47; F=310, p~3e-69; effect ~ -0.81 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_018=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_018=0 (a candidate treatment with overall positive effect).
Patients with feature_031=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_031=0.
Patients with feature_021=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_021=0.
Patients with feature_022=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_022=0 (a candidate treatment with positive effect).

analyses.result_summary: feature_018=1 mean pfs=3.64 vs 0 mean pfs=3.32; F=238, p~2e-53; main effect +0.32 months.
feature_031=1 mean pfs=3.32 vs 0 mean pfs=3.52; F=104, p~2e-24; effect -0.20 months.
feature_021=1 mean pfs=3.30 vs 0 mean pfs=3.49; F=74, p~9e-18; effect -0.19 months.
feature_022=1 mean pfs=3.60 vs 0 mean pfs=3.41; F=41, p~2e-10; main effect +0.19 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS regressing pfs_months on all features simultaneously, the strongest independent predictors are feature_015 (positive), feature_014 (negative), feature_023 (negative), feature_011 (negative), feature_006=squamous (negative), feature_016 (positive), feature_017 (negative), feature_025 (positive), feature_018 (positive), and feature_001=current (negative).

Most other features (feature_002, feature_004, feature_007, feature_008, feature_009, feature_010, feature_012, feature_013, feature_019, feature_020, feature_022, feature_026, feature_027, feature_029, feature_030, feature_033) have no independent effect on pfs_months once the strong predictors are accounted for.

analyses.result_summary: OLS R^2 = 0.894. Coefficients (sorted by |t|): feature_015 +0.168/unit, feature_014 -1.10/level, feature_023 -1.42, feature_011 -0.91, squamous -0.72, feature_016 +0.89, feature_017 -0.073/unit, feature_025 +0.46/unit, feature_018 +0.32, current-smoker -0.55, feature_031 -0.20, feature_032 -0.0004/unit (p~1e-28). All p<<1e-20 except feature_032.

In the same multivariable model, feature_002 (p=0.04, |coef|<0.02), feature_005 (p~7e-9, coef=-0.11), feature_028 (p=0.06), feature_021 (p=0.47), feature_022 (p=0.96), feature_012 (p=0.39), feature_020 (p=0.74), feature_027 (p=0.88), feature_004/008/009/010/013/019/026/030/033 (all p>0.3) show no meaningful independent additive effects. feature_022's univariate +0.19 is fully explained by smoking confounding (it correlates with non-former smoking).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022's univariate positive association with pfs_months disappears after stratification by smoking status (feature_001), implying it is a confounded baseline correlate rather than a treatment.
feature_012 (prevalence 50.0%, near-balanced) is randomly assigned with respect to baseline characteristics, suggesting it is a treatment, but its overall main effect on pfs_months is null.

analyses.result_summary: Within smoking strata, feature_022 effect on pfs: never -0.025 (p=0.60), former -0.008 (p=0.87), current +0.033 (p=0.76). All null. The univariate +0.19 was driven by f022's correlation with non-former smoking status.

Logit of feature_012 on all baseline features: only weak top associations (|z|<3.4 for all), consistent with random assignment. Main effect on pfs: -0.035 (p=0.08), null. Interaction screen: only feature_017 interaction is sig at p<1e-3 (coef=+0.018, t=3.6) — a small modifier of unclear clinical significance. feature_012 behaves as a randomly-assigned treatment with no efficacy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_018 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across baseline features. In particular, the effect is much larger in patients with feature_016=1 than in patients with feature_016=0.
feature_016 acts as a positive treatment-effect modifier: patients with feature_016=1 derive substantially greater pfs_months benefit from feature_018=1 than feature_016=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs ~ f018 + f016 + f018:f016. Interaction coefficient = +2.558, t=+41.88, p<1e-300. Stratified means: feature_016=0 — TE(f018) = 3.329 - 3.334 = -0.005 (p=0.80, NULL). feature_016=1 — TE(f018) = 5.766 - 3.213 = +2.552 months (p~3e-275). Treatment benefit is essentially confined to feature_016=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_016=1 patients, the treatment effect of feature_018 is further modified by feature_031: patients with feature_031=0 derive much larger pfs_months benefit from feature_018 than patients with feature_031=1 (i.e., feature_031 acts as a resistance modifier that abolishes feature_018's efficacy).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified within feature_016=1: feature_031=0 — TE(f018) = +4.644 (n_t=1237, n_c=2271, p~0). feature_031=1 — TE(f018) = -0.012 (n_t=1009, n_c=1851, p=0.88, null). The full interaction model in the f016=1 stratum gives a 3-way interaction t-statistic for (f018:f031) of -28.6 (p~3e-178). feature_031=1 abolishes the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within (feature_016=1 and feature_031=0), the feature_018 treatment effect is further attenuated by feature_028: patients with feature_028=1 derive no benefit from feature_018, while feature_028=0 patients keep the large benefit.

Within (feature_016=1 and feature_031=0), the feature_018 treatment effect is further attenuated by feature_005: patients with feature_005=1 derive no benefit from feature_018, while feature_005=0 patients keep the large benefit.

analyses.result_summary: In subgroup (f016=1 & f031=0), interaction f018:f028 has coef=-5.12, t=-12.59, p~1e-35. Direct stratification within (f016=1 & f031=0): feature_028=0 — TE(f018) = +4.83 (n_t=1191, n_c=2179); feature_028=1 — TE(f018) = -0.29 (n_t=46, n_c=92, p=0.51, null). feature_028=1 abolishes the benefit.

In subgroup (f016=1 & f031=0), interaction f018:f005 has coef=-4.86, t=-10.59, p~8e-26. Direct stratification: f005=0 — TE(f018)=+4.85; f005=1 — TE(f018)=-0.07 (n_t=39, n_c=68, p=0.88, null). feature_005=1 also abolishes benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete responder subgroup for feature_018 is defined by (feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0). Within this subgroup, feature_018 substantially increases pfs_months; outside this subgroup, feature_018 has no effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=3266, 6.5% of cohort): TE(f018) = 8.234 - 3.249 = +4.985 months (n_t=1154, n_c=2112, p~0). Outside subgroup (n=46734): TE(f018) = -0.0063 (p=0.76, null). Adding indicator (in_subgroup * f018) to the full multivariable model raises R^2 from 0.894 to 0.979 (delta R^2 = 0.085); coefficient on the interaction = +4.92, p~0. The four conditions are jointly necessary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No other binary feature (feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_021, feature_027) acts as a clinically meaningful treatment with a similar large heterogeneous effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Systematic interaction screens: feature_002 main TE=+0.009, no interaction with |t|>3 at p<0.001; feature_012 main TE=-0.035, only one interaction (with feature_017) at p<0.001 with tiny coef; feature_020 main TE=-0.025, no significant interactions; feature_021 main TE=-0.190 (small negative main effect), one interaction with smoking (coef +0.25, p=2e-8) but no large subgroup of benefit; feature_027 main TE=+0.004, no significant interactions. None resemble feature_018's pattern.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 is assigned approximately uniformly across the responder subgroup vs the non-responder subgroup (~35% in both), suggesting it is a randomly-or-uniformly-assigned treatment whose effect depends entirely on the biomarker subgroup, not biomarker-driven assignment.

analyses.result_summary: Receipt rate of feature_018=1 in responder subgroup = 35.3%; outside = 35.1% (Δ=+0.2 pp, NS). Logistic regression of feature_018 on all baseline features yields no |z|>6 (top: feature_025 z=-3.33, p=0.001, very weak). Treatment is essentially balanced across baseline; the entire conditional treatment effect is due to outcome heterogeneity, not assignment heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup (feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0 & feature_005=0), the feature_018 treatment effect is further (modestly) modulated by feature_015: older / higher feature_015 values show slightly larger treatment benefit than younger / lower values.

Within the responder subgroup, the feature_018 treatment effect is approximately constant across continuous baseline biomarkers (feature_017, feature_025, feature_032, feature_003, feature_010, feature_004, feature_008): all median-split treatment effects are within ±0.15 months of the +5.0 mean.

analyses.result_summary: Within max-resp subgroup, OLS pfs ~ f018 + feature_015 + f018:feature_015 gives interaction coefficient = +0.0156 per unit (t=3.24, p=0.001). TE by feature_015 quartile within max-resp: Q1 +4.68, Q2 +4.89, Q3 +4.79, Q4 +5.10. Real but small effect modification (~0.4 months across full feature_015 range).

Median-split TE within max-resp: feature_017 4.93/5.04, feature_025 5.06/4.90, feature_032 4.94/5.03, feature_003 5.07/4.90, feature_010 4.94/5.04, feature_004 4.96/5.01, feature_008 4.94/5.03. All differences ≤0.17, consistent with sampling noise; no further continuous modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four biomarkers defining the responder subgroup (feature_016, feature_031, feature_028, feature_005) are statistically independent of each other in the cohort (pairwise correlations ~0).

analyses.result_summary: Pairwise correlations among feature_016, feature_031, feature_028, feature_005 are all ≤0.015 in absolute value. Conditional prevalences match marginal: P(f031=1|f016=1)=44.9% vs marginal 44.7%; P(f028=1|f016=1)=4.07% vs marginal 4.93%. Biomarkers are independent; the responder subgroup arises as the intersection of four orthogonal conditions, with prevalence approximately 0.127 * 0.553 * 0.951 * 0.970 = 6.5% (matches observed 6.53%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: feature_018=1 (treatment) increases pfs_months by approximately +5.0 months on average, compared with feature_018=0, ONLY in the subgroup defined by (feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0). Outside this subgroup, feature_018 has no effect on pfs_months. Within the subgroup, the effect is essentially uniform across age and other continuous biomarkers, with a small ~0.4-month gradient across feature_015 (modest amplification with higher feature_015).

analyses.result_summary: Confirmed. In the subgroup (n=3266, 6.5% of N=50000): mean pfs with f018=1 is 8.234, with f018=0 is 3.249, difference = +4.985 months (t=63.6, p~0). Outside subgroup: difference = -0.006 (p=0.76). Adding the subgroup-treatment indicator to the multivariable model raises R^2 from 0.894 to 0.979.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Independent of treatment, the strongest baseline prognostic features for pfs_months (after multivariable adjustment) are: feature_015 (continuous, large positive), feature_023 (binary, large negative), feature_014 (3-level, monotone negative), feature_011 (binary, negative), feature_016 (binary, positive — also serves as treatment biomarker), feature_006=squamous (negative), feature_001=current (negative), feature_017 (continuous, negative), feature_025 (continuous, positive), and feature_028 (binary, modest negative on its own; large effect-modifier in subgroup).

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS adjusted coefficients: feature_015 +0.168/unit (t=512); feature_014 -1.10/level (t=-230); feature_023 -1.42 (t=-208); feature_011 -0.91 (t=-122); feature_006=squamous -0.72 (t=-95); feature_016 +0.89 (t=+90); feature_017 -0.073/unit (t=-87); feature_025 +0.46/unit (t=+71); feature_018 +0.32 (t=+47, but average over enormously heterogeneous effect); feature_001=current -0.55 (t=-44); feature_031 -0.20 (t=-31); feature_032 -0.0004/unit (t=-11); feature_005 -0.11 (t=-5.8). All listed p<1e-9.
